discovery: The system will support both iOS and Android platforms.
For mobile app development, React Native (Expo) is preferred within a $25k–$50k budget and a 4-month timeline.
Custom API-first development strategy is recommended to support web and mobile without building two separate products.
Recommendation for custom build on a managed LMS/e-commerce backbone to support multi-instructor, hybrid monetization, and payouts within the $25k-$50k budget and 4-month timeline.

Primary stack recommendation details: Next.js (Frontend), NestJS or AWS Lambda (Backend/API), PostgreSQL (Database), Supabase Auth or Auth0 (Auth/RBAC), Stripe (Payments/subscriptions), S3 + CloudFront or Mux/Vimeo (Content storage/streaming), Postmark/SendGrid (Email/notifications), Retool or lightweight admin in Next.js (Admin tools).
Roles locked as Admin, Student, Instructor (Teacher).
Subscription initially unlocks all courses for complete access.
Data model flexible to allow individual course purchases in the future.
Hybrid monetization model with monthly and annual subscriptions, plus a la carte payments for courses.
Instructor payouts based on enrollment counts, structured as a revenue share or per-enrollment model.
Payments layer structured around products, entitlements, ledger events, and payout cycles.
ui_ux: The system will use a shared API and types across web and mobile applications to ensure consistency.
The React Native app will consume the same API as the web application, allowing for shared TypeScript types/components.

High-level screens/pages include: Public (Landing, course catalog/browse, course detail, pricing), Auth (signup/login, verify email, forgot password), Student (dashboard, course player, certificates, billing/subscription), Instructor (dashboard, course builder/manage, analytics, payout history), Admin (manage users/roles, courses/moderation, subscription plans, payouts, reports).
features_journeys: The platform will be a standard web and mobile app experience for both desktop and mobile users.
The focus is on standard streaming, progress tracking, and certificates without hard 'native-only' requirements.
The custom app is designed to handle subscriptions, one-off purchases, entitlement logic, and enrollment-based instructor payouts.
Admin actions: Manage users, courses/content, monetization, entitlements, payouts, audit/monitoring.
Student actions: Browse catalog, purchase subscriptions, access learning, track progress, manage account/billing.
Instructor actions: Create courses, handle publishing, view aggregated analytics, manage payouts and profile.
Role-based access control for students, instructors, and admins.
Subscription lifecycle management and entitlement for all courses.
Course delivery with progress tracking and certificate issuance.
Instructor payout calculations based on enrollments with admin-managed payout cycles.
Key product modules: Course authoring, Content delivery, Access & monetization, Learning tracking, Certification, Payouts, Admin system.
Students have the option to pay via platform-level subscription, per-course purchases, or both.
tech_stack: Preferred tech stack for web includes Next.js (React) and NestJS (Node) API.
The database choice is Postgres, with options for Supabase or AWS RDS.
Authentication and RBAC will be handled by Supabase Auth or Auth0.
Stripe will be used for handling payments, including subscriptions and one-time purchases.
Storage and media delivery will utilize S3 and CloudFront, with optional integration of Mux/Vimeo for video streaming.
The mobile application will be developed using React Native (Expo), leveraging the same API as the web application.

Confirmation of the tech stack configuration: Next.js, NestJS or AWS Lambda, PostgreSQL, Supabase Auth or Auth0, Stripe, S3 + CloudFront or Mux/Vimeo, Postmark/SendGrid, Retool or lightweight internal admin in Next.js.
open_questions: Confirmation is needed on whether there are any hard 'native-only' requirements or if a standard streaming + progress + certificates app is sufficient.
Question regarding preference for avoiding custom development and using a platform like WordPress versus a custom build.
When a user has an active subscription, should they get access to all courses or only a subset?
